app.name: ShogiHome
app.one_liner: An open-source, Electron-based shogi GUI with touch-friendly UI, USI engine integration, and advanced analysis features like SPRT testing and evaluation delta visualization.

screens.name: Engine & Analysis Settings
screens.purpose: Configure USI engines, resource allocation, and analysis behavior including logging and script execution workarounds.

screens.name: Main Shogi Board
screens.purpose: Primary interface for playing, analyzing, and studying shogi positions with engine assistance and visual feedback.
primary_actions: Launch parallel games or SPRT tests
Start engine analysis or opening book search
View move arrows with evaluation deltas

screens.name: Time Consumption Chart
screens.purpose: Visualize time usage per move during games or analysis sessions.

rust_commands.name: launch_parallel_games
rust_commands.purpose: Start multiple simultaneous shogi games for testing or analysis.

rust_commands.name: run_sprt_test
rust_commands.purpose: Execute Sequential Probability Ratio Test between two engine configurations.

rust_commands.name: save_game_state
rust_commands.purpose: Persist current board state, engine settings, and analysis results.

rust_commands.name: start_engine_analysis
rust_commands.purpose: Initiate analysis using a registered USI engine or opening book as a pseudo-engine.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add engine registration and settings screen
week 1: Display evaluation arrows with delta values
week 1: Implement core shogi board with drag-and-drop piece movement
week 1: Integrate basic USI engine communication
week 2: Add CPU/memory warning dialog for resource-heavy configurations
week 2: Add support for opening books as pseudo-engines
week 2: Enable saving and loading game states
week 3: Enable protocol logging toggle in developer settings
week 3: Support custom time chart layouts
acceptance_tests: CPU/memory over-allocation triggers a warning in the game setup dialog.
Evaluation deltas are displayed on move arrows when engine analysis is active.
Opening books can be selected as an alternative to engines for move generation.
Protocol logging can be enabled via developer settings to debug engine communication.
SPRT test runs correctly between two engine configurations and reports statistical outcome.
Time consumption chart renders accurately during gameplay.
User can drag and drop pieces on the main board using touch or mouse input.
